Description: Waterbean Coffee was the first specialty coffee shop in the Lake Norman area that offered pour-over, French Press, Aeropress, and over 5 different espresso single origin or blends for our customers to choose from.
Comments:
This is my second favorite of all the Waterbean locations (the Pineville location is still my number one). It feels so cozy, spread out, tons of seating, has a fireplace (not sure if it's real or just for decoration, but I like that it's there). Never seen it get overwhelmingly busy here. Quite lovely.

The Waterbean Special sandwich is amazing, and I continue to love their dedication to providing an abundance of loose-leaf herbal tea options. Waterbean was the first coffee shop I visited after moving to Charlotte in 2020 (man, it's been so long!), so they have a soft spot in my heart. I'm happy to see them expanded to the greater Charlotte area.

Comments: Right across from a light rail stop, so easy access for those who live on the line and don’t want to drive here. It’s in the bottom of an apartment building, so plenty of traffic during peak hours. Parking is free if you can find a street spot, which might be tough given that  apartment residents and their guests usually have those spots. There’s a paid very small gravel lot nearby though. Description: Large, international food hall with many family-run stalls, lots of seating, and easy self-ordering kiosks. Description: Community centered, family-owned, mercantile & café bar in Downtown Rock Hill, SC.
Comments: It's a massive general store full of cool trinkets, gifts, and a laid-back, folksy vibe. The kind of place you can wander through for a while and still find something new each time. Inside, there's also a coffee shop tucked into the corner, perfect for grabbing a drink while you browse. The whole area makes for a great day trip, with other spots like Knowledge Perk, Rock Hill Coffee, Amelie's Rock Hill, and plenty of local shops within walking distance. If you're looking to wind down, end your day at nearby Fountain Park with a good book and something warm to drink. You’ll feel like you’re in a movie, one of those they used to always cast Drew Barrymore in.

Place: Knowledge Perk Coffee Company | Fort Mill
Operating Hours: ["Monday: 6:30 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 9:30 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 9:30 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 9:30 PM", "Sunday: 9 AM - 6 PM"]
Description:
Come relax in our beautiful café with indoor and outdoor seating. We offer freshly roasted coffee, cold brew, full range of expert espresso beverages, and a variety of sweet and savory pastries and bagel creations.

Thursday-Friday from 4:00pm-9:30pm, and Saturday from 6:00pm-9:30pm, we turn into Knowledge Perk "After Hours," serving coffee, original cocktails, beer, and wine.

We are a coffee roaster and beans are available onsite or via our website for retail purchase. We have immersive, engaging programs and events for our community to grow in their knowledge and enjoyment of coffee. Give us a call or visit our website for more information. Better yet, drop by to see us in person!
Comments: The cinnamon rolls. My friend, the cinnamon rolls, they are exceptional. They only have a limited amount, usually available in the morning until they’re sold out, but the cinnamon rolls are marvelous. If you're able to withstand feelings of gluttony or social anxiety stemming from the thought that the barista is judging you (they're not), order two. Eat one right away, warm the other one up when you get home. That's how to properly evaluate a cinnamon roll. You must have it in both states, as each is valid in its own right.

We offer fresh roasted coffee, cold brew, a full range of expert espresso beverages, seasonal drink menu, and a variety of sweet and savory pastry and bagel creations.
All coffee is roasted in house and is available onsite or via our website for retail purchase. We have immersive, engaging programs and events for our community to grow in their knowledge and enjoyment of coffee. You can also rent out our café for your next event, or we can come to you with our coffee bus or catering options!
Give us a call or visit our website for more information. Better yet, drop by to see us in person!
Comments: The cinnamon rolls. My friend, the cinnamon rolls, they are exceptional. They only have a limited amount, usually available in the morning until they’re sold out, but the cinnamon rolls are marvelous. If you're able to withstand feelings of gluttony or social anxiety stemming from the thought that the barista is judging you (they're not), order two. Eat one right away, warm the other one up when you get home. That's how to properly evaluate a cinnamon roll. You must have it in both states, as each is valid in its own right.

Address: 7201 Matthews-Mint Hill Rd, Mint Hill, NC 28227
Operating Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 9:30 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 9:30 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 6 PM"]
Description:
Coffee shop by day and wine bar vibes at night. Check us out for natural wines, local craft beers, and a selection of handmade cocktails, as well as, light far served from open to close. We have a small but excellent selection of wines to go as well. With the largest selection of natural wines in Mint Hill.

Whether your thing is coffee, tea, boba, fresh hand-squeezed lemonade, beer, wine or anything in between this is the place to go for absolutely delicious beverages all day long.
Comments:
Sits in a converted house with some seating inside, but plenty outside, and all of it rather cozy. It’s a similar vibe to Brakeman’s in Matthews. Has that homey community gathering spot feel, in part because it’s literally in a home, but also because they host plenty of events and gatherings for various groups in the area. 

It's a relaxing spot, down to the easy listening/folk music they usually have playing. There are enough spots indoors near outlets to hang out here for remote work if you come in the morning when you'll have your pick of the place. And there's plenty of parking behind the building.

But most importantly, they have cinnamon rolls 🙏. Not the cute bite sized kind, proper fully formed cream cheese frosting cinnamon rolls, and they are sublime. Sometimes they switch things up and do special flavor cinnamon rolls. I've caught a peach cobbler flavored one here before. It was otherworldly. Made me sing hallelujah.

Address: 14825 Ballantyne Village Way Suite 135, Charlotte, NC 28277
Operating Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 6 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Coffee Republic in Ballantyne Village is the best coffee shop near you! Enjoy artisanal coffee, fresh pastries, and a welcoming ambiance. Whether grabbing a quick cup or relaxing with friends, our café is your go-to spot for quality coffee and treats.
Comments:
Lots of food and pastry options that all look great, including cinnamon rolls from time to time that are huge. But not a ton of comfortable seating options. Downstairs is all high-top bar-style seating with your back facing the entrance and checkout area, so everyone coming in is walking directly behind you. The TikTok feng shui guy would say that’s not good design.

There’s an upstairs area too, but it’s super compact. A big table, a couch, and some smaller tables and chairs are all squeezed in tightly. If you go early and claim a good seat, it could work as a place to get things done. But if not, I wouldn’t call it cozy or comfortable.

If you come here to work or study and can't find a spot, whip around the corner to Panera Bread. It’s big and you’ll almost definitely find seating. Sunflour Bakery, Summit Coffee, and Starbucks are all nearby too. Ballantyne has its own little third place thing going on.

Description: We were the first specialty coffee shop in Lake Norman and are now expanding to Belmont. Our goal is to create a space where you can have business meetings, study, hang out, or simply grab a quick cup of coffee to-go. We aim to be your home away from home.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 1 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 9 AM - 3 PM"]
Description:
Wake is an exclusive, yet inclusive Coffee & Champagne Bar. Specialty drinks and cocktails are served with style and finesse. Whether you're here for an intimate date or with a group of friends our staff will serve you with hospitality and excellence, The Wake Way.

Our staff is well qualified in the techniques and quality ingredients used to create our crafted drinks, coffee and cocktails. Enjoy an afternoon or evening of charcuterie, high end pastries with your choice of coffee, champagne, wine, or beer. Our vision was to create an exclusive experience for our community through hospitality while highlighting Charlotte's hidden gem, Mtn. Island Lake.
Comments:
Small space but they make good use of it to fit seating. They have small cubicle style areas near the back where you can work/study, but they’re paid spaces. So paid mini co-working spaces, can’t just pop into one. But they also have limited general seating in the front of the store that’s open to all. They have coffee, tea, treats (ice cream, pastries), and a bar. 

They have cinnamon rolls that they get from Sam’s club, cream cheese frosting. I know this because I’m plugged into the cinnamon roll scene of greater Charlotte. It’s something of an area of expertise for me. They’re marvelous cinnamon rolls, better warm, but amazing regardless. They’ve got some unique tea options, sourced locally.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 11 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 11 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 11 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 11 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 12 AM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 12 AM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 11 PM"]
Description:
The country of Yemen is unapparelled to any other place on this Earth. From its breathtaking scenery to its history that dates back to the beginning of man. Yemen is the homeland of our family. As first-generation immigrants, we wanted to honor our heritage.

Yemen is where coffee was invented and later flourished in global trade. Roasting and serving coffee is something that is inclusive of all people and cultures, and brings people together. Our coffee is grown from altitudes over 3,000 ft in a mountainous village called Haraz, where our namesake originates.
Comments: Immaculate vibes. One of the few places open later so if you need to really get some work done or study, this is a good place to hangout. Very popular date spot though, so it can be tough to find a space to sit if you go during peak hours. There’s two parking options: behind the store and across the street on Hawthorne (behind 1124 Central Ave). Parking is in general pretty tough though.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 4 PM"]
Description: A stylish locally owned coffee shop with cozy built-in nook seating, warm earthy tones, and a calm, design-forward atmosphere. Good for coffee, quiet conversation, or posting up for a bit in a space that feels more curated than typical. The semi-private bench alcoves make it stand out.
Comments:
If there were awards for most creatively designed small coffee shops in Charlotte, this spot would win, or at least be nominated. There’s only a seating few spots inside the coffee shop and a few more in the lobby of the larger building. However, the ones inside the shop are cool. 

There’s built-in alcove seating along a wall, with long cushioned benches tucked into recessed nooks and framed by beaded curtain dividers. It feels cozy, slightly private, and a little loungey without being fully closed off. There’s also an interesting book hanging out on a shelf there for you to read. Oh and the baristas are very nice, and they have excellent taste in music. A good selection of 2008-2020ish bangers.

Also they have cinnamon rolls 🙌🏾🙏🏾❤️. They’re small but mighty in flavor. Idk if they bake or buy then but I’m always happy to see cinnamon rolls. And they properly steep their teas, loose leaf, no bag in your cup. Respect.

Comments: I hate the mall, so you'll never catch me hanging out here, but it's very nice and extremely convenient to access. You don't have to go deep into the mall, it's on the edge of the food court and has its own entrance. Also, there's a play area outside with a large TV, so parents love this place. Sometimes they'll even put on a kids show for a family night sort of feel.

Comments: This is inside a larger office/retail building that has plenty of seating in the lobby area. If you go in the morning, get the French Toast, it’s good.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 4 PM"]
Description: Sunflour Baking Company is the Charlotte bakery & cafe you have been looking for! Come visit our five locations to get a taste of our fresh brewed coffee or small batch, homemade pastries and breads.Since 2009, Sunflour Baking Company has been a local favorite in the Noda neighborhood as well as for customers all around Charlotte and beyond. People all over the country order our Cake Jars every day!We invite you to come to visit our local cafes. They are all great spots to hang out, get work done, catch up with friends or enjoy some quiet time. We are sure you will love our bright, friendly interiors and of course our pastry case.
Comments: They have what I consider to be the best cinnamon rolls in Charlotte. I suggest getting two, eat one right away, and save the other for eating at home after warming it up. As a general rule, I’d encourage you to refrain from offering your opinion about a cinnamon roll until you’ve had it both cold and warm. Things can taste rather different heated up, flavors coming out, for better or worse. They can sell out of cinnamon rolls pretty quickly sometimes though, if you show up after like 11 AM, no guarantees. The sandwiches are also pretty good. Be warned though, the whole menu is expensive. Like, I’ll go in here and buy 2 things and get asked for $28 and be just flabbergasted. But the cinnamon rolls, they wash it all away. So it’s worth it. The gigantic rice krispy treat they have sometimes also slaps.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: This cheerful gathering place draws a crowd for its pastries, light fare & coffee drinks.
Comments: This place is huge. You have the entry level with plenty of seating, but also an upstairs that can easily seat 30 people and a downstairs (that’s sometimes blocked off for private events) that can fit a good chunk of people as well. The only thing about Uptown is if you're driving, you'll have to pay for parking. But if you're on the rail trail or walking distance, definitely make the trek over here.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 5 PM"]
Description: Stylish coffee bar offering pour-overs, cold brew & espresso, tea, food, and baked goods.
Comments:
It's giving hipster warehouse millennial core. Come with your man buns, thrifted denim jackets, skinny jeans, Chelsea boots, and a playlist full of Mumford & Sons, The Lumineers, Bon Iver, Vampire Weekend (Big Blue is one of my all-time favorites), and Arcade Fire.

The "Hex" in the name isn't a witchy thing, it refers to the hexagon shape of a hornet’s nest, which is a nod to Charlotte’s “Hornet’s Nest” nickname. So, calm down Evangelicals and Catholics, you can patronize this place and keep your conscious clear (I am devoutly Christian, albeit free of any denomination, so I get to make that joke).

This is in Camp North End, which is a huge plus in terms of location. Parking is free, there's a gravel lot out back, another lot on the far end by Tipsy Pickle, and street parking. Please don't pay for parking here, it's free. Anyways, they have some real fancy named teas and they’re all incredible. If you get it “for here”, they give you a nice cup and this fancy kettle pot thing to pour it from.

The food here is good, they give you a lot of it for the price. It’s not small, cute coffee shop meals. It's a full restaurant style plate of food. The Hex Special is my go-to, lots of food to justify the high price. Oh, and they have orange flavored cinnamon rolls sometimes 🔥💯. 

Lots of windows, gives the place a bright and energetic feel. Excellent access to outlets as well. Great music playlist (if you're a millennial, or older Gen Z such as myself), good vibes. The outdoor seating is also solid, there's Wi-Fi outdoors from Camp North End, and even a spot with outlets to stay charged. On a warm day, it's a great spot to post up and work. 

Overall, this is an awesome day trip for remote workers, Camp North End has a lot to explore. Start at Hex, walk around after work and explore. Solid day.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 9 AM - 5 PM"]
Description: Deli sandwiches, breakfast sandwiches, coffee, fine wine, craft beer.
Comments:
Great spot to work from during the day. Only gets crowded during lunch, but even then, there's a lot of seating indoors and outdoors so you can almost always find a spot. Tea, coffee, deli, and a convenience store inside. Top tier remote work spot. Everything you need is there.

It becomes a bar in the evenings (or early afternoon for the day drinkers). If you sit at the bar, there’s outlets underneath the table. This is also a great place to hang out on a warm day. There's a large outdoor patio. You could bring a group and have plenty of space to sit. Maybe a book club, something similar. I know for a fact groups of SouthPark mom's meet here all the time on warm days to play Mahjong.

Description: Local bakery chain serving Charlotte and nearby neighborhoods since 2009. Fresh from scratch pastries, breads, coffee, sandwiches, and more. Whether you are looking for a quiet place to work and get fast free WiFi, or need to place a large catering order, our bakery can't be beat. Looking for lunch? We serve fresh made sandwiches every day of the week. Visit us on East 7th Street today, or shop online and ship fresh baked goods nationwide.
Comments: They have what I consider to be the best cinnamon rolls in Charlotte. I suggest getting two, eat one right away, and save the other for eating at home after warming it up. As a general rule, I’d encourage you to refrain from offering your opinion about a cinnamon roll until you’ve had it both cold and warm. Things can taste rather different heated up, flavors coming out, for better or worse. They can sell out of cinnamon rolls pretty quickly sometimes though, if you show up after like 11 AM, no guarantees. The sandwiches are also pretty good. Be warned though, the whole menu is expensive. Like, I’ll go in here and buy 2 things and get asked for $28 and be just flabbergasted. But the cinnamon rolls, they wash it all away. So it’s worth it. The gigantic rice krispy treat they have sometimes also slaps.

Description: Stylish cafe for gourmet coffee, sandwiches & pastries in a chic space with lots of seating options.
Comments:
The indoors is connected to a hotel which has additional seating if it’s crowded in the main area. Great vibes here. Plenty of remote workers huddled inside. It's a common recommendation among remote workers and students in Charlotte for good reason. The vibes. The vibes are A+. 

They have cronuts, and sometimes cinnamon rolls 🔥. The only thing is, it's in Uptown, so you're paying for parking if you drive. Worth it though, at least once to check it out. The upstairs area above the stadium style seating has some excellent tucked away spots, great for feeling cozy, and outlet access excellent all throughout the store. 

You can sit in the hotel as well if need be. It’s one of Charlotte’s best coffee shops in terms of space, vibes, aesthetic, and convenience for students and remote workers wanting to stay a while. They even have some bigger tables for groups, so your book club can hang here too.

Description: This funky store with a variety of goods houses an all-day eatery & deli with a bar & live music.
Comments:
Plenty of seating, both indoors and in a covered outdoor section, great sandwiches, coffee, a bar, various convenience store offerings. Eclectic, punk sort of vibes in terms of aesthetic. Feels lived in, authentic. You'll see plenty of remote workers on their laptops during the day. 

When you make a deli order, they ask your name then announce it over a loudspeaker when its ready. Gave me grade school nostalgia, being called to the office. Good luck if you have a tough name, I use a simplified version of mine to avoid embarrassment. The biggest challenge here is outlet access, so come charged up.

Description: Popular spot offering draft brews & deli sandwiches amid a convenience store, plus outdoor seating.
Comments: Indoor seating is mostly bar style, high chairs, but there's additional outdoors. Great spot for a warm day. Convenience store, deli, bar, coffee, and really cool punk/alt vibes. The convenience store has a wider selection of random snacks and drinks than a gas station typically would have, which I appreciate. Parking is free behind the store but limited, especially during peak hours.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Popular spot offering draft brews & deli sandwiches amid a convenience store, plus outdoor seating.
Comments:
This place is spacious, with indoor and outdoor seating on the main floor, plus an upstairs area with more seating and a small patio. Very alt-punk/rock aesthetic, which I personally love since I grew up in Seattle. It feels like the kind of spot the crew from a 2000s coming-of-age film (the ones who get bullied by the popular kids and listen to Radiohead, Dashboard Confessional, Mayday Parade, My Chemical Romance,  Boys Like Girls, All Time Low, and Yellowcard) would hang out at, talking through their trauma and how much they want to "get out of this town." 

And I mean that in the best way possible (I may have just listed some of my favorite bands). This place is awesome. The sandwiches are great. The music is on point. I don’t even bother with headphones unless it’s packed. Truly a standout when it comes to vibes. I'm surprised it's not busier, I consider it a hidden gem in South End. There's a lunch crowd, but outside of that on weekdays during work hours it's pretty chill. 

For remote work, there’s some access to outlets upstairs, and 1-2 downstairs by the vending machine, but not a lot overall, so come charged up. If you’re a morning person, you can practically have the whole place to yourself if you show up early. It doesn’t get busy until the afternoon. The outdoor seating is also great and plentiful.

Parking is the biggest challenge here. There’s free parking near the store, but it’s limited. Street parking and nearby lots are options, though not always free. South End is designed for walking and light rail access, which is great unless you’re coming from another neighborhood and need your car. Not complaining though, just making a note.

I know it's a coffee shop, but I'm a tea person, so as you read my comments on this site, that's what you'll see me reviewing drink wise. 

Parking is free in the angled spots in front of the store but time limited (2 hours). There's paid lots in the area if you want to stay a while. There's a Common Market within walking distance, great spot for lunch, and also to hangout when it's warm outside, plenty of outdoor seating.

Description: Popular gathering place for craft coffee drinks & tea with outdoor seating on the front lawn.
Comments:
Sits in a converted home, very cozy, very welcoming, very warm. Between outdoors, the first floor of the house, and the upstairs area, there's a good amount of seating. My favorite spots are the seats on the street side of the house in the covered patio area. If you're a "my life is a movie" type of person, they're a great spot to post up and watch the world go by, sit by the window, people watch, watch cars and businesses skate by, read or write a book. And there's solid outlet access in that area which surprised me, it's just a little cooler on cold days so bring a jacket.

Overall, for the size of the house, they do well with indoor and outdoor seating. But it's such a popular spot that I'd say still be warned if you're going during peak hours. Remote workers love this place, as do students, as do book clubs, as do the after-church crowd, so go early to secure the best seating. The parking situation is a gravel lot behind the building, but it can be challenging during peak times. There are about 6-8 spots right behind the shop and a handful more directly in front. There's free parking in the area though if you're willing to walk a bit, just drive around.

It's a great atmosphere for working remotely with a cozy rustic vibe. Also, a great spot for meeting friends or just vibing. It's everything a third place is supposed to be. Coffee and tea are great, and they have great pastries as well, including orange flavored cinnamon rolls sometimes and cookies from Honeybear Bake Shop (some of the best in Charlotte). Great spot.

Comments:

Parking is free, which is rare for South End, making it one of the better picks for those driving from different neighborhoods looking for a hangout spot. It's walking distance (literally seconds) from Vicente as well, so you can park once and hit two spots.

Description: A fun coffee shop and gathering space featuring a pool table, comfortable indoor and outdoor seating, themed event nights, puzzles and various board games.
Comments:
This place is in the bottom of an apartment building and across from a bunch of stores. As a result, it's popular just about every day of the week. If you want to be social and talk to people, it'd be a great spot to post up. They have a pool table and various events put on by the apartment complex (karaoke night, trivia night, stuff like that) from time to time. 

There’s very limited parking directly in front of the store but plenty more in the light rail parking garage. The staff will validate your ticket if you use the garage. Seating by outlets can be tough, maybe 2-3 spots have access, so come charged up.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 12 AM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 12 AM", "Sunday: 10 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: A brewery, coffee bar, American eatery & fermentation lab share their products all under one roof.
Comments:
Hot spot in South End for a younger crowd of remote workers. Coffee bar during the day, brewery in the evenings. Parking is free, it's a gravel lot you kind of have to keep an eye out for. Doesn't stick out from the street.

If you come here in the morning, you can have nearly the whole place to yourself. It doesn't get busy until the afternoon (South End lunch crowd). They have a handful of booths that each have outlet access, as well as some outdoor tables with outlets nearby. 

This and the Common Market in South End are my favorite remote work spots in the area, specifically because they're not that crowded in the mornings. You can also order via QR code here and have it brought to your table, so if you're an introvert, you can walk in, talk to literally no one, sit down, plug in, order, and just vibe man, just vibe. Surrounded by people, yet still alone. Or I mean, if it's a warm day, this is also a great date spot. Tons of outdoor seating, you can find a corner, have a private conversation.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 12 AM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 12 AM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Airy, laid-back brewpub pouring house beers paired with wood-fired pizzas & other modern bar bites.
Comments:
Cool. That’s the word I’d use to describe this place. It feels a little hidden being upstairs, which somehow makes it even better. The music, the eclectic decor, the paintings, the random plants, the murals, everything about it just works. It’s cool. Or hip. That word fits too.

You could honestly film one of those “cool young people spreading peace, love, and unity while hanging out with a diverse group of friends” commercials here. And I mean that in the best way possible. I don’t really know how else to describe it. It’s just cool. The only thing that's not cool is parking. It's NoDa, you'll be lucky to find a free street spot, else its paid lots.

The pizza is good, and daytime on weekdays it isn't that busy, so it's a solid place to work remotely. There's a handful of spots with outlet access, and plenty of seating. I don't drink beer, but when I do, I prefer Dos Equis I've been told what they serve here is really good. They also serve loose leaf tea if you get it "for here", which is much appreciated by tea lovers such as me.

Operating Hours: ["Monday: 9 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 9 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 9 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 9 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 9 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 10 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Bookseller chain stocking housebrand eReader, plus a broad selection of titles for adults & kids.
Comments:
They have a good set of the comfy couches both downstairs and upstairs, but mostly upstairs. There’s maybe 1-2 couches in a corner downstairs, but upstairs they line the window that runs the length of the building. Excellent spots to read. Premium coziness.

Downstairs there’s a Starbucks with a bunch of seating. Only 3-4 spots that are near outlets though, so charge your laptop if you’re coming to work or study. Idk why but the Starbucks inside this Barnes & Nobles has different and way better food, drink, and snack options than normal Starbucks. 

This location has marvelous cookies. The salted caramel one is 10/10. And the hot tea options are way more expansive than normal Starbucks. Harney & Sons tea options, quality stuff. So as weird as this may sound, the Starbucks inside this Barnes & Nobles is a hidden gem in and of itself.

Perhaps the only downside here is it gets super busy. This place is flooded with students meeting their tutors and study groups weekdays after school. Although you can still usually find a spot somewhere. But after church on Sunday? Forget it. This place is packed. So, just time your visit well. I’ve found, if you have remote  work flexibility, it’s plenty tranquil 9 AM - 5 PM on weekdays. It’ll just be you, the staff, a few house moms, and other remote workers. 

The parking lot is small, but you can park across the street in the gravel lot by the apartments. That’s retail parking as well. Oh and there’s a really good ice cream place in this complex. Golden Cow Creamery. Check it out.

Known for consistent quality, attention to detail, and baristas who can help you choose something new, it is a reliable stop whether you are grabbing a quick coffee or exploring more adventurous seasonal drinks.
Comments: The cinnamon sugar bagel is very good, they give you a generous amount of cream cheese. Like, almost too much, which I consider to be a good thing. Also they have a solid tea menu, not an overwhelming amount of choices, but the ones they have are a step above your standard Earl Grey, English Breakfast, and Jasmine Green teas. I'm a tea person, so any spot with good tea automatically becomes a favorite of mine. If you get the tea "for here" they give you a nice mug and a little glass holder thing with extra tea and a plate and sometimes even a gold colored (not actual gold) spoon. Very quaint. Very posh. Very "demure". I know I'm not using that word right. I don't care.

I suspect the Caribou and Bruegger's staff both think the other will do it, and neither does, so it never gets done lol.

Place: Amélie’s French Bakery & Café | Park Road
Operating Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: This charming gathering place draws a crowd for its pastries, light fare & coffee drinks.
Comments:
One of my favorite third places in Charlotte. In fact, it was my first third place here. This location is spacious, bright, and thoughtfully designed. The ground floor has large tables that make it easy for groups to meet, and you’ll often see book clubs, church groups, and D&D squads gathered around. Even with all that activity, there always seems to be a seat for solo visitors.

The slightly raised seating area that runs the length of the building is perfect if you come alone. It’s lined with two-top tables and counters and feels a bit tucked away from the main floor. You can walk in, see families and groups filling the space, and still know there’s a quiet corner waiting for you.

Outlet access is excellent. Along the booth-style seats in the raised seating section, there’s a slim black strip beneath the padded backs with outlets. They are easy to miss, but they’re there. Someone clearly put effort into making sure people could stay a while.

Now for story time. I moved to Charlotte during the COVID-19 pandemic, which meant nearly everything worth exploring was closed. When things started reopening, smaller spots struggled with distancing, but larger ones like this Amelie’s found a way. They removed a few tables and still let people sit safely apart. After more than a year of being confined to my studio apartment, this became my first “just sit somewhere and be out of the house” spot. I would bring my laptop and a book, work remotely, watch shows, read, and just exist somewhere that wasn’t home.

I came here three or four times a week. The staff eventually started predicting my order, which is partly why I began branching out to other places. I felt I needed to be more adventurous. But this Amelie’s will always be special to me. It was my home away from home. I made my first friends in Charlotte here and saw so many others doing the same. People working, reading, studying, and talking. This spot embodies what a third place is supposed to be.

The food and pastries are alright. Nothing life-changing, but nothing bad either. The cinnamon roll is just okay, it’s gotten better. It used to be terrible, but since it’s not a French pastry, I give them a pass. The coffee is reliable, and the tea options are good. Pro tip, you can order a full pot of tea even though it’s not listed on the display menu. Between that, the pastries, and the welcoming atmosphere, everything here invites you to stay a while.

It may not be the original Amelie’s, but it’s my original. I pray it never goes away.
